merged_events.event_description: Consular relations established at Copenhagen when U.S. consul Hans Saabye received an exequatur from the Danish government.

evidence.quote: Consular relations were established on or about June 9, 1792, when the U.S. consul at Copenhagen, Hans Saabye, received an exequatar from the Danish government.

merged_events.event_description: U.S. Legation at Copenhagen established when Chargé d'Affaires Henry Wheaton presented his credentials to the Danish government.

evidence.quote: The U.S. Legation at Copenhagen was established on September 20, 1827, when Chargé d'Affaires Henry Wheaton presented his credentials to the Danish government.

merged_events.event_description: U.S. Legation at Copenhagen closed following the outbreak of war between the United States and Germany.

evidence.quote: The U.S. Legation at Copenhagen was closed on December 20, 1941, following the outbreak of war between the United States and Germany.

merged_events.event_description: U.S. Legation at Copenhagen reopened following Denmark's liberation from German occupation during World War II.

evidence.quote: On June 16, 1945, the U.S. Legation at Copenhagen was reopened following Denmark's liberation from German occupation during World War II.

merged_events.event_description: U.S. Legation at Copenhagen raised to an Embassy when Ambassador Josiah Marvel, Jr. presented his credentials to the Danish government.

evidence.quote: The Legation at Copenhagen was raised to an Embassy on March 18, 1947, when Ambassador Josiah Marvel, Jr. presented his of credentials to the Danish government.

matched.notes: Exact date and status match. Both record consular relations established 1792-06-09 at Copenhagen.
Exact date and status match. Both record U.S. Legation established 1827-09-20 at Copenhagen.
Exact date and status match. Both record closure of Legation on 1941-12-20 following outbreak of war with Germany.
Exact date and status match. Both record reopening of Legation on 1945-06-16 following Denmark's liberation.
Exact date and status match. Both record elevation to Embassy on 1947-03-18.
